summary_sections.title: Lesson Overview
summary_sections.content_html:
Overview

This lesson covers the fundamental measures of center used to analyze and summarize data sets. You'll learn how to calculate and apply the mean, median, and mode, and understand when each is most appropriate.

summary_sections.title: Definitions and Concepts
summary_sections.content_html:
Key Definitions

What is the definition of 'Standard deviation (s)'?

"Standard deviation (s)" means: The square root of variance; measures the typical distance of observations from the mean in the original units of measurement. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Variance (s²)'?

"Variance (s²)" means: The average of the squared deviations from the mean, calculated using n-1 in the denominator for sample data (Bessel's correction). The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Range'?

"Range" means: The difference between the maximum and minimum values; simple but highly sensitive to outliers. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Degrees of freedom'?

"Degrees of freedom" means: In the sample variance formula, n-1 degrees of freedom accounts for the fact that the sample mean is estimated from the data. The other options describe different concepts.

What is the definition of 'Coefficient of variation'?

"Coefficient of variation" means: Standard deviation divided by the mean, expressed as a percentage; useful for comparing variability across datasets with different units or scales. The other options describe different concepts.

summary_sections.title: Outliers and Resistant Measures
summary_sections.content_html:
Outliers and Resistant Measures

This description refers to "Range".

summary_sections.title: Real-World Applications
summary_sections.content_html:
Real-World Applications

Example: Which term is described by: "The average of the squared deviations from the mean, calculated using n-1 in the denominator for sample data (Bessel's correction)"?

This description refers to "Variance (s²)".

Example: Which term is described by: "Standard deviation divided by the mean, expressed as a percentage; useful for comparing variability across datasets with different units or scales"?

This description refers to "Coefficient of variation".
